channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: California sues to stop Trump from imposing sweeping tariffs
item.description: SACRAMENTO, Calif. (AP) — California Gov. Gavin Newsom sued the Trump administration on Wednesday, challenging the president's authority to impose sweeping tariffs that have set off a global trade war.
item.dc:creator: SOPHIE AUSTIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: GOP Rep. Elise Stefanik is considering a run for governor of New York
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Rep. Elise Stefanik, a member of House Republican leadership and onetime nominee for U.S. ambassador to the United Nations, is considering a run for New York governor, according to a person close to her. The person was granted anonymity to discuss internal deliberations.
item.dc:creator: SEUNG MIN KIM and ANTHONY IZAGUIRRE, Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: How war, money and the quest for discovery entwined the US government and universities
item.description: The showdown between the Trump administration and Harvard University is spotlighting bare-knuckled politics and big dollar figures.
item.dc:creator: Adam Geller, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: 10 guards charged over the fatal beating of a New York inmate, including 2 with murder
item.description: UTICA, N.Y. (AP) — Ten New York prison guards were charged Wednesday in connection with the fatal beating of a 22-year-old inmate last month — including two charged with murder. It's the second time a group of correctional officers in the state was indicted for a death behind bars this year.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL HILL and DAVE COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Arson attack probe at Pennsylvania governor's mansion looking into suspect's hatred of Josh Shapiro
item.description: HERSHEY, Pa. (AP) — Authorities investigating why a man set fire to Pennsylvania Gov. Josh Shapiro’s official residence are probing whether the suspect was motivated by the Democrat's Jewish faith or positions on Israel’s war in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: MARK SCOLFORO and JOHN SEEWER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Student accused in Dallas school shooting walked toward group in hall and opened fire, injuring 4
item.description: DALLAS (AP) — The student accused of injuring four in a shooting at a Dallas high school was let into the building through an unsecured door and then walked down a hallway toward a group of students, opening fire on them and then appearing to take a point-blank shot at one, according to an arrest
item.dc:creator: JAMIE STENGLE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AmeriCorps members who respond to disasters and help nonprofits are let go in DOGE cuts
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — Young volunteers who respond to natural disasters and help with community projects across the U.S. have been discharged as a result of the Trump administration 's campaign to shrink government workforce and services.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Watch: Crews rescue stranded hiker 'death gripping' cliff on California trail
item.description: “She was able to send an emergency text to 911 via her Garmin-type emergency communications device,” the Instagram post said.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, NewsNation

item.title: Hertz says some customers' personal info exposed in breach
item.description: While the company did not say how many may have been impacted, they did identify the type of information that may have been exposed.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Wife of 'Ghost Adventures' star agrees to plead guilty to conspiracy to murder
item.description: The wife of “Ghost Adventures” star Aaron Goodwin agreed to plead guilty to conspiring to murder her husband as part of a plea deal and could face up to 10 years in prison, documents show.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KRON4

item.title: Conservation group names Mississippi River 'most endangered,' cites proposed FEMA cuts
item.description: American Rivers, a nonprofit environmental advocacy group, said the Mississippi River in recent years has faced “increasingly frequent and severe floods” that have damaged homes and businesses and worsened the health of the river, which provides drinking water for 20 million people.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, KRON4

item.title: ACLU sues Defense Department school system over banning race- and gender-related books
item.description: The American Civil Liberties Union has sued the Department of Defense’s school system for children of military families, asserting that the removal of race- and gender-related books and curricula violated students’ First Amendment protections against government censorship.
item.dc:creator: OLIVIA DIAZ, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: New England serial killer fears merit 'review and investigation' after remains found across 3 states: expert
item.description: Human remains found in Connecticut, Rhode Island and Massachusetts between March and April warrants "review and investigation," according to criminal defense attorney Matthew Mangino.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: One dead in multi-boat crash during Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama
item.description: A fatal boat crash during a Major League Fishing tournament in Alabama is currently under investigation.
item.dc:creator: Austin Franklin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Slave descendants fight to protect their threatened island community at Georgia's highest court
item.description: SAVANNAH, Ga. (AP) — Georgia's highest court waded Wednesday into a fight between Black landowners and local officials who have weakened long-standing protections for one of the South's last Gullah-Geechee communities founded by freed slaves.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Menendez brothers' family accuses DA of violating victim protection law after showing graphic photo in court
item.description: Family of Lyle and Erik Menendez are accusing Los Angeles District Attorney Nathan Hochman's office of violating Marsy's Law after graphic crime scene images are shown in court.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Boasberg moves to hold Trump administration in contempt over deportation flights
item.description: A federal judge found probable cause Wednesday to hold the Trump administration in criminal contempt for willfully disobeying his order to immediately halt deportations under the rarely used Alien Enemies Act and turn around any airborne planes. U.S.
item.dc:creator: Zach Schonfeld, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Company retaliates after asphalt deal goes wrong: Police
item.description: What was supposed to be a typical paving job culminated in a gas station owner trapped in his store and calling the police.
item.dc:creator: Tanner DeLeon/ KFOR, NewsNation

item.title: DOJ announces civil suit against Maine over transgender athletes
item.description: Attorney General Pam Bondi on Tuesday announced a civil lawsuit against Maine’s Department of Education for violating Title IX, the federal law against sex discrimination that the Trump administration has said prohibits transgender students from participating in girls’ and women’s sports in schools.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Migdon, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Seth Rogen calls out Silicon Valley's Trump support in cut awards show remarks: Report
item.description: “And it’s amazing that others [who have been] in this room underwrote electing a man who, in the last week, single-handedly destroyed all of American science,” Rogen, 43, said.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, NewsNation

item.title: Olympic event to be played in front of iconic movie backdrop at 2028 Games, organizers say
item.description: LA28 officials have unveiled an even more detailed look at the venues that will be used during the 2028 Olympics.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Unmanned shrimp boat washes ashore on Florida beach, puzzling investigators
item.description: A 50-foot shrimp boat drew onlookers after it ran aground without its crew on a Florida beach over the weekend, according to local law enforcement.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: AP Was There: A truck bomb rips through a federal building in Oklahoma City in 1995
item.description: OKLAHOMA CITY (AP) — EDITORS NOTE: On April 19, 1995, a former U.S. Army soldier parked a rented Ryder truck loaded with a powerful bomb made of fertilizer and fuel oil outside a federal office building in Oklahoma City. The blast at the Alfred P.
item.dc:creator: SEAN MURPHY, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: White House removes wire spot from press pool
item.description: The White House is making changes to which outlets are included in the press pool covering President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Dominick Mastrangelo, Fox 5

item.title: Maryland senator to visit El Salvador to check on mistakenly deported man
item.description: Maryland Sen. Chris Van Hollen (D) said he is traveling to El Salvador to check on the status of Kilmar Abrego Garcia, a deported legal resident.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Starbucks introducing new dress code for baristas, a move protested by some
item.description: Baristas at your local Starbucks will soon be sporting a new, more consistent look after the company announced a new dress code this week.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Massachusetts man in gas mask pepper sprays officers in courthouse near Boston, video shows
item.description: Authorities in Massachusetts said Nicholas Akerberg, 28, attacked people inside a courthouse in a Boston suburb with pepper spray on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Wink Martindale, game show host and Elvis Presley interviewer, dies at 91
item.description: Wink Martindale, the genial host of such hit game shows as “Gambit” and “Tic-Tac-Dough” who also did one of the first recorded television interviews with a young Elvis Presley, has died. He was 91.
item.dc:creator: By BETH HARRIS Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Santa Fe sheriff releases body camera footage of Gene Hackman investigation
item.description: Newly-released body camera footage shows the confusion and questions investigators had after actor Gene Hackman and his wife Betsy Arakawa were found dead in their home.
item.dc:creator: Marilyn Upchurch, WGN 9

item.title: FL representative says he'll go to El Salvador if wrongly deported man isn't returned this week
item.description: U.S. House Representative Maxwell Frost (D-Fla.) says he'll join other Democrat lawmakers planning to go to El Salvador if a man mistakenly deported isn't returned this week.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Georgia man arrested over alleged threats to kill Tulsi Gabbard
item.description: A Georgia man was arrested after federal authorities said he made numerous death threats against Director of National Intelligence (DNI) Tulsi Gabbard, the Justice Department announced Monday.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Fortinsky, Fox 5

item.title: Federal judge drops contempt case against ICE agent over arrest outside Boston courthouse
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — A federal judge has dismissed a contempt case against an agent for the U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement whose arrest of a man during his trial sparked criticism among the law enforcement community in Boston.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Trump administration freezes $2.2 billion in grants to Harvard over campus activism
item.description: The federal government says it’s freezing more than $2.2 billion in grants and $60 million in contracts to Harvard University, since the institution said Monday it won’t comply with the Trump administration’s demands to limit activism on campus.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Watch: Elephants at San Diego park form protective circle during earthquake
item.description: Watch: Elephants at the San Diego Zoo Safari Park surrounded the younger elephants in a protective "alert circle" when a 5.2 magnitude earthquake struck the region Monday morning.
item.dc:creator: Anna Ashcraft, ABC 4

item.title: Massive bluefin tuna catches halted at NC pier after officials step in, manager says
item.description: For several days a huge bluefin tuna were caught at Jennette's Pier in Nags Head, but the bluefin fun came to an end after an official visited last week, the pier said.
item.dc:creator: Rodney Overton, WGN 9

item.title: Video shows mother pick up baby and run during Southern California earthquake
item.description: A quick-thinking San Diego County mother saved her baby from a 5.2 magnitude earthquake in eastern San Diego County.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KRON4

item.title: 2022 NCAA woman of the year, family members killed in upstate New York plane crash
item.description: A private plane that crashed in upstate New York over the weekend was carrying six members of a close-knit family of physicians and distinguished student-athletes on a trip to the Catskills for a birthday celebration and the Passover holiday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Fox 5
